text:
Here are the results:

**Merged PRs closing non-'done' Linear issues:**

| PR # | Linear Issue | Issue State |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| 1009 | ENG-183 | todo |
| 1010 | ENG-130 | todo |
| 1022 | ENG-183 | todo |
| 1026 | ENG-153 | todo |
| 1030 | ENG-120 | in_progress |
| 1040 | ENG-136 | canceled |
| 1042 | ENG-135 | in_review |
| 1050 | ENG-203 | canceled |
| 1053 | ENG-215 | backlog |
| 1057 | ENG-143 | backlog |
| 1058 | ENG-104 | todo |
| 1069 | ENG-113 | in_progress |
| 1078 | ENG-132 | canceled |

**Total count: 13 merged PRs.**

Notable observations:
- **ENG-183** appears twice (PRs #1009 and #1022) — two merged PRs claiming to close the same issue.
- 4 of the 13 issues are in **canceled** state, meaning the work was abandoned yet a PR still got merged claiming to close them.
- 2 are still **in_progress**, 1 is **in_review**, and the rest are in **backlog** or **todo** — none have been moved to **done**.

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'linear_issues'"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"SELECT pr.number, pr.closes_linear, li.state
FROM github_pull_requests pr
JOIN linear_issues li ON pr.closes_linear = li.id
WHERE pr.state = 'merged'
  AND li.state != 'done'
ORDER BY pr.number"}
